page: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at 2.0 GHz with 4 physical cores and a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration under a shared allocation model. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core) and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor features a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is characterized by strong large-block write bandwidth (top 25%) and average read bandwidth. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks (gzip and bzip3) perform strongly, though multi-threaded decompression and database operations show weaker relative performance, placing in the bottom 25%.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is a notable tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained multi-core execution. However, the inclusion of high-speed NVMe storage and top-tier single-core performance provides a cost-efficient profile for compute-focused tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. This server is best suited for single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, Redis caching, and small-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: The Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 32 MB L3 cache. The hardware supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance (top 10% in stress-ng) and strong large-block memory write bandwidth, though multi-threaded decompression and database operations are weaker. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, single-threaded workloads, lightweight Redis caching, and small-scale model inference, balancing high single-core capabilities against shared CPU resource limits.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 75 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring AMD EPYC Genoa processors, fast NVMe storage, and exceptional single-core performance for targeted workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Genoa architecture with 4 shared vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
75 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AES hardware acceleration
Best suited for single-threaded processing, Redis caching, and lightweight inference
